examples: Ole missis, she tole me I got to go an' git dis water an' not stop foolin' roun' wid anybody.
It was she who tole me the news.
Though people have often laughed at stories tole by seamen.
The tole is embossed, hammered and formed, and then attached to stems fashioned of copper wire.
The green bits, the stems and leaves, began as porcelain too but were so prone to breaking that the artist now constructs them of painted metal, or tole.
